content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges: [(0, 1, 0), (1, 2, 1), (0, 2, 2), (0, 3, 7), (5, 9, 14), (0, 6, 15), (1, 8, 15), (3, 9, 19), (7, 8, 19), (0, 7, 21), (3, 5, 27)], we need to find when node 1 and node 5 are first connected.

We will scan each edge and collect the time t whenever u and v match the two queried nodes (in either order).

First, we have (0, 1, 0) and (1, 2, 1), so we collect t = 0.
Next, we have (0, 2, 2) and (0, 3, 7), so we collect t = 0 and t = 7.
Then, we have (5, 9, 14) and (0, 6, 15), so we collect t = 14.
After that, we have (1, 8, 15) and (3, 9, 19), so we collect t = 15.
Finally, we have (7, 8, 19) and (0, 7, 21), so we collect t = 19.

Now we have collected all the times t when the queried nodes are connected. We will sort them in ascending order and take the minimum value.

t = 0
t = 7
t = 14
t = 15
t = 19

The minimum time when node 1 and node 5 are first connected is t = 14.

Answer: 14
